This increase of s593,s10 on rail-
roads is a larger increase than has
been made upon this class of prop
erty by any other county in Wash
ington in the past two years.


There are three classifications in
assessments of railroads, viz: Track,
rolling stock and sidetrack. OF the
10 counties having railroads of the
first class, or transcontinental lines,
only two-Lincoln and Yakima-have
higher rate of assessment than Spo-
kane upon main track and right of
way, two-Snohomish and King-are
lower than Spokane. Only one
Pieree-is higher than Spokane on
sidetrack; Yakima is the same, and
all others are lower than Spokane.
On rolling stock. all are the same, ex-
cept Franklin and Kittitas, which are
lower.


The above will no doubt surprise
those people who have e the
Spokesman-Review's bitter attacks
upon Assessor Stewart regarding his

undisputed figures from the records,
and they are ample proof of the
claim made by Mr.. Stewards friends
that he is the most lied-about man
in Spokane county.

crease upon the railroads, the present
assessor has increased the assess
ment of some SO other firms and cor
porations, including the Washington
Water Power company, the gas COM
pany and the Review Publishing
company, between one and a half and
two million dollars, but has put nc
increase upon the homes. Instead,
he has actually reduced the assess
ments upon more than a thousand
